formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 12 Computer Science — Linked List (Theory) in JSON format.

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Insertion Operation",
    "formula": "Insert node after a given position: prev -> new_node -> next",
    "variables": "prev (previous node), new_node (new node to be inserted), next (next node)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To insert a node after a given position in a linked list."
  },
  {
    "name": "Deletion Operation",
    "formula": "Delete a given node: prev -> next",
    "variables": "prev (previous node), next (next node)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To delete a node from a linked list."
  },
  {
    "name": "Traversal of Linked List",
    "formula": "Start at the head of the list and traverse each node until the end is reached.",
    "variables": "head (first node in the list)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To visit each node in a linked list."
  },
  {
    "name": "Time Complexity for Linked List Operations",
    "formula": "Insertion: O(n), Deletion: O(n), Traversal: O(n)",
    "variables": "n (number of nodes in the list)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To determine the time complexity of linked list operations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Linked List Data Structure",
    "formula": "A linked list is a linear collection of data elements whose order is not given by their physical placement in memory.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To define a linked list data structure."
  },
  {
    "name": "Circular vs. Linear Linked List",
    "formula": "Circular Linked List: NULL points to the first node, Linear Linked List: NULL points to the last node.",
    "variables": "NULL (null pointer), first node",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To distinguish between circular and linear linked lists."
  },
  {
    "name": "Advantages of Linked List",
    "formula": "Flexible memory management, Efficient insertion and deletion, No extra memory required for storing pointers.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To list the advantages of using a linked list."
  },
  {
    "name": "Disadvantages of Linked List",
    "formula": "Random access is not allowed, Extra memory required for storing pointers, Time-consuming traversal.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To list the disadvantages of using a linked list."
  },
  {
    "name": "Types of Linked List",
    "formula": "Singly Linked List, Doubly Linked List, Circular Linked List.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "To classify linked lists into different types."
  }
]
```

Note: There are no mathematical formulas as such in the Linked List chapter, but the above JSON array includes key statements/laws, operations, and data structures related to the chapter.
